Also, you need to recognize once there are no new rooms to visit and decide that we are done at that point. Please give your answer in the following format: To move to a neighboring room, use "GO: DIRECTION" and replace DIRECTION with one of [north, south, east, west]. To stop the exploration, answer with "DONE" instead. Omit any other text.
Here is an example:
You are in the Kitchen. Currently available directions: south, west. What is your next instruction?
GO: west
You have made a step and entered a Lobby. Currently available directions: east, north. What is your next instruction?
GO: north
...
You have made a step and entered a Bedroom. Currently available directions: south. What is your next instruction?
DONE
Let us start. You are in the Utility room. Currently available directions: south, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: south
You have made a step and entered Attic. Currently available directions: north, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: east
You have made a step and entered Home office. Currently available directions: west, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: east
You have made a step and entered Dining room. Currently available directions: west, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: east
You have made a step and entered Garage. Currently available directions: west. What is your next instruction?
GO: west
You have made a step and entered Dining room. Currently available directions: west, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: west
You have made a step and entered Home office. Currently available directions: west, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: west
You have made a step and entered Attic. Currently available directions: north, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: north
You have made a step and entered Utility room. Currently available directions: south, east. What is your next instruction?
GO: east
You have made a step and entered Billiard room. Currently available directions: west. What is your next instruction?

message.reasoning:
We need to explore all rooms with fewest changes, i.e., perform a traversal covering all rooms (presumably a graph of rooms). We need to recognize when no new rooms left. So far visited rooms: Utility room (start), Attic, Home office, Dining room, Garage, Billiard room. Need to know other rooms not visited; maybe there are more (e.g., Kitchen, etc). We need to explore from Billiard room: only direction west leads back. West from Billiard room likely returns to Utility room? Let's infer map: Utility east leads to Billiard room. So Billiard's west to Utility. So returning to Utility is backtracking.

We've visited all we know? Unknown rooms: maybe "Living room", "Bedroom", etc. Not visited yet. We need to explore from Utility via south again? At Utility currently we have south and east. East already done to Billiard. South leads to something else (maybe a new room). So we should go south to discover new area.

Thus GO: south.

message.reasoning:
We need to explore all rooms with fewest moves. We need to understand map layout from traversals so far.

We have visited: Utility (start), went south to Attic, east to Home office, east to Dining room, east to Garage, west back to Dining room, west to Home office, west to Attic, north back to Utility, east to Billiard room. From Billiard room only west is available (invalid earlier south). So go west.

We need to continue exploring other rooms not yet visited. Which rooms have we not visited? Unknown list. Let's track graph.

Rooms and connections:

Utility: south to Attic, east to Billiard room (just discovered). Also likely north/west? Unknown.

Attic: from Utility north? Actually Attic had south? Wait we went from Utility south to Attic, then Attic had north, east. So Attic connections: north back to Utility, east to Home office (we went east). Possibly others unknown.

Home office: from Attic east to Home office, also west back to Attic, east to Dining room. So Home office connections: west Attic, east Dining room.

Dining room: from Home office east to Dining, also west back, east to Garage, maybe west to Home office etc.

Garage: only west back to Dining. So dead end.

Billiard room: from Utility east to Billiard, only west back to Utility.

Thus visited all? Unknown other rooms: maybe "Living room", "Bedroom", "Kitchen", etc. We haven't seen them. Need to explore new directions from rooms that have other exits not yet taken.

Check each visited room for unexplored exits:

Utility: we have used south and east. Any other directions? At start we had south, east only. So no more.

Attic: we have used north (back) and east (to Home office). Any other directions? At Attic originally had north, east. So done.

Home office: had west, east. We used both (west to Attic, east to Dining). Done.

Dining room: had west, east. We used both (west to Home office, east to Garage). Done.

Garage: only west. Done.

Billiard room: only west (to Utility). Done.

Thus all rooms visited? Might be other rooms not reachable? Maybe there are more rooms reachable from Utility via north or west not yet known because we haven't seen those directions. But Utility only listed south and east currently. So map appears fully explored. Thus we should DONE.
